channel.title: Workshops on Daoyin Chuan
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

item.title: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha
item.description:
We will be holding the Daoyin Chuan 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha on August 17th and 18th.  Friday (17th) night will be from 7:30pm to 10pm and Saturday (18th) will be from 9:30am to 5pm.  We will go to a local restaurant for our lunch break so be sure to bring money or a brown bag.  Either way we will be eating lunch together.  The location for the workshop is at The Grateful Pose (Yoga Center) at 845 N 400 E, Bountiful, UT.

The cost will be $110.00 for the workshop, if paid in advance.  Friday night at the door will $30.00 and Saturday at the door will be $95.00.  Attached is a registration form with more details: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha.

There will be a recording of the workshop that will be available in September/October time-frame for a minimal cost.  This years workshop will be a great learning experience in a very nice and peaceful environment.  Looking forward to seeing you there.

item.title: Time to focus on sitting.
item.description: Yesterday while playing basketball during lunch I tore the plantar fascia off my heel bone. I tried to accelerate forward after playing defense. Heard and felt a load pop in my left heel and then excruciating plain and couldn’t walk on it. So I am unable to stand or walk for a while. This will give me plenty of time to practice sitting Baduanjin and the microcosmic orbit meditation. I am also glad that Ha Sifu showed us this year some sitting Yiquan postures. Appears to be really good timing, at least for me. Also, I’ve added Sifu Fong Ha’s instructions to the digital download store.

item.title: Gleaning's from Ha Sifu
item.description:
This weekend is the Ha Sifu workshop for Daoyin Chuan. Seems to be a feeling of excitement surrounding the workshop this year. Looking at those attending it appears to be a more intermediate/advanced group. Some are martial artists from different styles, even external arts. We are all looking forward to learning from a master this weekend.

Friday Night

First thing Sifu Fong Ha reminded me is that to learn others’ center is to discover my own. A lesson that he has been teaching me for years.

  • It is all about equilibrium (dong jing).

  • Best way to feel/learn equilibrium is to be as you are, especially in stillness.

  • Small and slow is best to maintain awareness of equilibrium.

  • Always touch the equilibrium and never the weight of others.

  • Do not allow other’s to put the weight on you.

  • During zhan zhuang be aware of how your body achieves and maintains equilibrium. How you body doesn’t fall down.

Simple lessons but for me a little challenging in overcoming old habits and maintaining the feeling.

Saturday

  • Get really good at the ordinary to become extraordinary. Meaning standing and walking.

  • Some walk with coins on the feet. Even tea cups full of water.

  • In push-hands remain connected, no separation.

  • In push-hands, touch is to remain light (like touching an iron-hot steel) and in-touch with equilibrium and not the weight.

  • In push-hands, allow both yourself and partner to reach equilibrium before expanding from the center.

Note: This post is organic. It will be changing and growing over then next few days.
